meaning: v.下沉,沉淀,平息,减退,衰减
examples: She waited nervously for his anger to subside.
Weak foundations caused the house to subside.
Sometimes, the ground at an oil field may subside as oil is removed.
But as the economy goes forward, these issues will subside and rates will come down.
Inflation is running well above the target rate and unlikely to subside until June.
